title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Guided Program Studio
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds accessible human and machine report system from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible human and machine report system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Report Information Architecture
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 9482 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Structured Alternative
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-iz8n8r" that uses case 1502 to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Structured Alternative.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Structured Alternative into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Structured Alternative, keep Structured Alternative connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-iz8n8r
# Competency: bookbot-structured-alternative.
# Case 1502: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_iz8n8r(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iz8n8r = "iz8n8r-r8n8zi-1502"
    assert evidence_variant_iz8n8r
    evidence_axes_iz8n8r = (
        "corpusiz8n8r",
        "policyr8n8zi",
        "revision1502",
        "commandiz8n8r1502",
        "reportr8n8zi1502",
        "test1502iz8n8r",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iz8n8r)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"
